title: Journey to the End of the Night
author: Louis-Ferdinand Céline

setting.note: Céline's night belongs to the poor northern edge of Paris: the boulevards around Place de Clichy and the grey working-class banlieue just over the old city wall, where a disillusioned doctor treats patients who cannot pay. Bardamu wanders far first, through the trenches, a French African colony and Ford's Detroit, but the book keeps returning to this rim of the city and ends there. The map anchors that Paris and the suburb of Clichy behind it; the overseas detours are marked as excursions beyond the setting.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Louis-Ferdinand Céline, first published 1932. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.

palette.note: The blue-black of the night the title keeps walking into, and of the smoke over the banlieue. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
